Hunter A. Dyer

Security Researcher & Reverse Engineer

I break binaries, build ML systems that understand machine code, and write about the intersection of security research and deep learning. Currently exploring diffusion models for binary analysis.

Read the Blog View Projects Resume

Featured Projects

Binary Patch Diffusion

Predicting masked x86-64 assembly via diffusion models

An ML system that uses continuous diffusion to predict masked spans of x86-64 assembly instructions. Trained on 518k examples extracted from 5,675 system ELF binaries. Features a DiT architecture with adaLN-Zero conditioning.

machine-learningreverse-engineeringdiffusionx86-64

RE-Router

Automated binary analysis routing framework

A framework for routing binary analysis tasks to the appropriate tools and techniques. Automates common reverse engineering workflows with intelligent decision trees.

reverse-engineeringautomationbinary-analysis

NN-Hiding

Steganographic neural network weight concealment

Research into hiding neural network weights within other neural networks using steganographic techniques. Explores the intersection of model security and information hiding.

machine-learningsteganographysecurity
